name: voice_clone_grandparent_scam_voiceprint_bank_callback
premise: Fraud operations director Dana Carr must decide whether to require voiceprint enrollment for outbound high-value wire callbacks after deepfake voice clones succeeded in three grandparent scams last week. Elder services advocate Gabe Park warns enrollment burdens cognitively declining customers who lack smartphones. Skipping enrollment preserves access but may allow clones to defeat security questions. Regulators have not ruled on biometric consent for seniors under guardianship.

agents.name: Dana Carr
agents.identity: I am the fraud operations director accountable for elder financial protection, ADA accommodations in phone banking, and loss metrics tied to synthetic audio attacks.
agents.goal: I want strong callbacks without excluding elders who cannot complete biometric enrollment workflows.

agents.name: Gabe Park
agents.identity: I am an elder services advocate who trains bank staff on supported decision-making and who documents guardianship edge cases where consent is contested.
agents.goal: I want human-in-branch verification for high wires until voiceprint consent rules are clarified, even if that slows fraud response.

choices: I will mandate voiceprint enrollment for all high-wire callbacks with paper fallback appointments within twenty-four hours, accepting queue delays during the rollout week.
I will skip voiceprint and require in-person or video-notarized callbacks for wires over a threshold, accepting higher branch load and residual clone risk for urgent remote families.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Wire desks are jammed before holiday travel peaks. Dana Carr must choose between mandatory voiceprint with paper fallback or in-person notarized callbacks above threshold. What does Dana Carr do?
